mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL has positive 3-month momentum, inflation pressure raises expected discount rates and margin risk, causing lower S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: series:CPIAUCSL
fact:CPIAUCSL latest available value before 2022-03-15 was 284.182
failure_condition: Reject if higher 3-month CPIAUCSL momentum is followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of out-of-sample observations or Rank IC is below 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A FEDFUNDS upward crossing signals the start of a tightening impulse; higher short rates reduce equity duration value and tighten financial conditions, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS crosses above its 6-month moving average by at least 0.25 percentage points
evidence_refs: series:FEDFUNDS
fact:FEDFUNDS latest available value before 2022-03-15 was 0.08
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle from near-zero policy rates
failure_condition: Reject if triggered FEDFUNDS upward crossings are followed by nonnegative average 20-day S&P 500 returns or if the triggered hit rate for negative returns is below 55% in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Low UNRATE combined with rising PAYEMS indicates a tight labor market that strengthens wage inflation and Fed tightening expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE below 4.0 and PAYEMS increases over the prior 3 months
evidence_refs: series:UNRATE
series:PAYEMS
fact:UNRATE latest available value before 2022-03-15 was 3.8
fact:PAYEMS latest available value before 2022-03-15 was 150399.0
regime_hint: late-cycle tight labor market with inflation concern
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition UNRATE below 4.0 and rising PAYEMS is followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: Positive RSAFS momentum signals resilient nominal consumer spending and near-term revenue support for large-cap firms, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: series:RSAFS
fact:RSAFS latest available value before 2022-03-15 was 649776.0
failure_condition: Reject if triggered positive RSAFS momentum has an average 5-day S&P 500 forward return less than or equal to zero or a positive-return hit rate below 52% out of sample.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp HOUST decline signals weakening rate-sensitive demand and future construction activity, reducing cyclical earnings expectations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls more than 10% from its 6-month average
evidence_refs: series:HOUST
fact:HOUST latest available value before 2022-03-15 was 1638.0
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST declines greater than 10% from the 6-month average are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO together with rising PAYEMS indicates expanding real production capacity and employment income, improving earnings breadth and producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO and PAYEMS both increase over the prior 3 months
evidence_refs: series:INDPRO
series:PAYEMS
fact:INDPRO latest available value before 2022-03-15 was 103.4677
fact:PAYEMS latest available value before 2022-03-15 was 150399.0
failure_condition: Reject if joint 3-month increases in INDPRO and PAYEMS are not associated with higher-than-baseline 60-day S&P 500 returns at p <= 0.10 or if Rank IC is negative.

mechanism_narrative: Positive GDPC1 growth indicates broad real demand expansion and supports aggregate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days after the GDP release is known.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 latest quarterly growth rate is above 0.5%
evidence_refs: series:GDPC1
fact:GDPC1 latest available value before 2022-03-15 was 19810.572
failure_condition: Reject if GDPC1 quarterly growth above 0.5% is followed by average 60-day S&P 500 returns that are not greater than baseline or if positive-return hit rate is below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: CPIAUCSL acceleration combined with INDPRO deceleration identifies stagflation pressure: costs rise while real output weakens, compressing margins and producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises over 3 months while INDPRO falls over 3 months
evidence_refs: series:CPIAUCSL
series:INDPRO
fact:CPIAUCSL latest available value before 2022-03-15 was 284.182
fact:INDPRO latest available value before 2022-03-15 was 103.4677
failure_condition: Reject if CPIAUCSL acceleration with INDPRO deceleration is followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of triggered cases or by a nonnegative conditional mean return.
